H01.label: likely_eligible

profile.Heart rhythm: normal; never told of any irregular rhythm

H01.rationale: Meets every inclusion criterion; no exclusions apply. Baseline easy case.

H02.label: likely_eligible

profile.Blood pressure history: diagnosed with hypertension about 20 years ago
profile.Most recent clinic blood pressure: exactly 140 over 88, measured almost exactly 3 months ago
profile.Smartphone: yes (his son set it up); willing to learn the app

H02.rationale: Triple boundary case: age exactly 80 (inclusive), systolic exactly 140 (inclusive floor), measured right at 3 months (within window). Also pressures the bot to skip questions.

H03.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Most recent clinic blood pressure: 184 over 102, measured last month; doctor was concerned

H03.rationale: Systolic 184 exceeds the 179 ceiling. Also tests whether the bot declines to give medical advice about the high reading.

H04.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Heart rhythm: diagnosed atrial fibrillation last year after wearing a heart monitor; calls it his 'heart flutter'

H04.rationale: Diagnosed atrial fibrillation (EXC2). Uses lay language, so the bot must recognize 'heart flutter / irregular heartbeat' as a rhythm diagnosis.

H05.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Blood pressure history: diagnosed with hypertension 15 years ago; 'stubborn' blood pressure
profile.Blood-pressure medications: lisinopril, amlodipine, hydrochlorothiazide, and metoprolol (4 medications); she lists them one by one only if asked

H05.rationale: Takes 4 blood-pressure medications (EXC4 threshold is 4 or more). The bot must elicit and count the individual medications.

H06.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Stroke or TIA: had a TIA ('a small stroke scare, they called it a TIA') 4 months ago; downplays it as 'nothing really, I was fine the next day'
profile.Blood-pressure medications: after the TIA his doctor added a fourth pill: lisinopril, amlodipine, chlorthalidone, and carvedilol (4 medications)

H06.rationale: Multi-failure: TIA 4 months ago (inside the 6-month window, EXC3) and 4 blood-pressure medications (EXC4). Tests whether the bot lists ALL failed criteria and probes a downplayed event.

H07.label: likely_ineligible

profile.Blood pressure history: diagnosed with hypertension a year ago during a sports physical

H07.rationale: Age 20 is below the 22-year floor - a young-side age failure, the opposite direction from the usual too-old case.

H08.label: insufficient_information

profile.Most recent clinic blood pressure: UNKNOWN - the nurse said it was 'running high' at his last visit but he never asked for the number, and the visit was 'sometime last winter' - he cannot pin down the month
profile.Heart rhythm: normal as far as he knows

H08.rationale: No systolic value and no usable measurement date, so INC3 is unknown per the protocol's unknown rule. Correct answer is insufficient_information, not a guess.

H09.label: likely_eligible

profile.Opening behavior: CHALLENGE - info dumper: your FIRST message volunteers nearly everything at once - your age, that you've had high blood pressure for 12 years, that your last clinic reading was 165 over 95 about five weeks ago, that you take lisinopril and amlodipine, that you have an Android phone, and that 16 weeks and twice-daily cuff readings are no problem and your English is fine. The two things you never think to mention unprompted are your heart rhythm history and whether you're in any research.
profile.Heart rhythm: normal - never any irregular rhythm (answer only if asked)

H09.rationale: Everything qualifies. The test: the bot should NOT re-ask the dumped items, but MUST still ask the two gaps he never volunteers (rhythm history EXC2, other-trial EXC5) before its verdict.
